ground_truth.title: 4 Different Ways to Arrange Your Living Room Furniture
ground_truth.author: Andy Weier
ground_truth.main_content: To me, a functional floorplan is the foundation of a well-designed home. I wanted to show you how with a little creative thinking and problem solving you can arrange the furniture four different ways in a living or family room. When I start working with a new client I usually walk into a space and see the potential of what it could be. Not in a judgmental way! I'm not picking apart what is wrong with the cosmetics of a house or room so much as I immediately begin to visualize how this space can be best utilized for the homeowner. There are a few things to take into consideration like doorways, openings into a room, window placement, closets, stairways, etc. You do need to be mindful of traffic patterns to make accessing a space and the furniture within fairly easy. Here is a living/family room measuring 23'-0" x 13'-0", with 10'-0" ceilings, a fireplace, five windows, and two doorways stepping down into the room. Below are four different furniture arrangements. Option 1 creates a more intimate conversational area with two small-scale sofas opposite each other, with end tables centered around a circular coffee table. Option 2 maximizes seating options for entertainment with the TV mounted above the fireplace, placing a full-sized sofa and console table on the opposite wall. Option 3 focuses on family using a sectional for primetime movie watching, game night, or just hanging out. Option 4 takes a more traditional approach with a clean open layout highlighting the great natural lighting provided by the five windows, with the TV on a console table opposite the fireplace and two full sized sofas across from each other.
with: Here is a living/family room measuring 23'-0" x 13'-0", with 10'-0" ceilings, a fireplace, five windows, and two doorways stepping down into the room.
In this particular layout, the goal was to create a more intimate conversational area.
In this option, the primary use of the room would be for entertainment. We wanted to maximize seating options and offer flexibility by creating multiple conversation areas.
Here, our focus is family. Using the ever-popular sectional we've provided seating for primetime movie watching, game night, or just hanging out.
So there you have it! I've shown you four different floor plan options for one room, but the possibilities are endless!
